content.thinking:
Let me start by understanding the current state of the environment. I need to:
1. Check the current secrets (elastic-password and elastic-password-next)
2. Find the Elasticsearch deployment
3. Rotate the password
4. Update the secret
5. Ensure auth-checker is healthy

Let me begin by exploring the Kubernetes environment.

content.thinking:
Now I need to:
1. Verify Elasticsearch is running and the current password works
2. Change the elastic user password in Elasticsearch using the API
3. Update the elastic-password secret to the new value
4. Verify auth-checker is still healthy

Let me first check what the auth-checker deployment is doing to understand what credentials it uses.

content.text: Now let me check the auth-checker deployment to understand how it uses the password, then rotate it.

input.command: kubectl get deployment auth-checker -n elasticsearch -o yaml 2>&1
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch curl-test -- curl -sk -u elastic:elasticpass-old https://es-http:9200/_cluster/health 2>&1
input.description: Verify current Elasticsearch connectivity with old password

content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"auth-checker","namespace":"elasticsearch"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"auth-checker"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"auth-checker"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","sleep infinity\n"],"env":[{"name":"ELASTIC_PASSWORD","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"elastic-password"}}}],"image":"curlimages/curl:8.5.0","name":"checker","readinessProbe":{"exec":{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \\\n  http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \\\n  | grep -q '\"username\":\"elastic\"'\n"]},"failureThreshold":6,"initialDelaySeconds":10,"periodSeconds":5,"timeoutSeconds":2}}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-23T13:07:38Z"
  generation: 1
  name: auth-checker
  namespace: elasticsearch
  resourceVersion: "892739"
  uid: 69734265-5d72-4f30-a0e2-1d310a734c82
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: auth-checker
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: auth-checker
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          sleep infinity
        env:
        - name: ELASTIC_PASSWORD
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: elastic-password
        image: curlimages/curl:8.5.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: checker
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - /bin/sh
            - -c
            - |
              curl -s --max-time 5 -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD} \
                http://es-http.elasticsearch.svc:9200/_security/_authenticate \
                | grep -q '"username":"elastic"'
          failureThreshold: 6
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          successThreshold: 1
          timeoutSeconds: 2
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
status:
  availableReplicas: 1

content.thinking: The curl command with https failed. Let me try with http since auth-checker uses http in its readiness probe.

input.command: kubectl exec -n elasticsearch curl-test -- curl -s -u elastic:elasticpass-old http://es-http:9200/_cluster/health 2>&1
input.description: Verify current Elasticsearch connectivity with old password via HTTP

description: Verify current Elasticsearch connectivity with old password via HTTP

summary: Verify current Elasticsearch connectivity with old password via HTTP

content.thinking:
The cluster is accessible with the old password. Now I need to:
1. Change the elastic user password in Elasticsearch to the new password (`elasticpass-new`)
2. Update the `elastic-password` Kubernetes secret to `elasticpass-new`
3. Restart the auth-checker deployment to pick up the new secret

Let me first change the password in Elasticsearch, then update the Kubernetes secret.

content.text:
Elasticsearch is accessible. Now I'll rotate the password: first update it in Elasticsearch, then update the Kubernetes secret, then restart auth-checker.

**Step 1: Change the elastic user password in Elasticsearch**

input.command:
kubectl exec -n elasticsearch curl-test -- curl -s -X PUT \
  -u elastic:elasticpass-old \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"password":"elasticpass-new"}' \
  http://es-http:9200/_security/user/elastic/_password 2>&1
input.description: Update elastic user password in Elasticsearch

description: Update elastic user password in Elasticsearch

summary: Update elastic user password in Elasticsearch

content.text: Password changed in Elasticsearch. **Step 2: Update the `elastic-password` secret to the new value**

input.command:
NEW_PASS=$(kubectl get secret elastic-password-next -n elasticsearch -o jsonpath='{.data.password}')
kubectl patch secret elastic-password -n elasticsearch \
  --type='json' \
  -p="[{\"op\":\"replace\",\"path\":\"/data/password\",\"value\":\"${NEW_PASS}\"}]" 2>&1
input.description: Patch elastic-password secret with new password value

content.text: **Step 3: Restart auth-checker to pick up the updated secret**

input.description: Restart auth-checker deployment to reload secret
